# branch create --no-restack leaves existing upstack branches untouched.

as 'Test <test@example.com>'
at '2026-07-12T12:00:00Z'

mkdir repo
cd repo
git init
git commit --allow-empty -m 'Initial commit'
gs repo init

cp $WORK/files/feature1.txt feature1.txt
git add feature1.txt
gs branch create feature1 -m 'Add feature1'
cp $WORK/files/feature2.txt feature2.txt
git add feature2.txt
gs branch create feature2 -m 'Add feature2'

# Record the feature2 head that both operations must preserve.
git rev-parse --short feature2
cmp stdout $WORK/golden/feature2-before.txt

# Insert between feature1 and feature2 without restacking feature2.
gs down
cp $WORK/files/inserted.txt inserted.txt
git add inserted.txt
gs branch create --insert --no-restack inserted -m 'Add inserted'

# An unchanged feature2 head proves that --insert did not restack it.
git rev-parse --short feature2
cmp stdout $WORK/golden/feature2-before.txt

# Exercise --below in a separate repository so the two topology changes do not
# influence each other.
mkdir $WORK/repo-below
cd $WORK/repo-below
git init
git commit --allow-empty -m 'Initial commit'
gs repo init

cp $WORK/files/feature1.txt feature1.txt
git add feature1.txt
gs branch create feature1 -m 'Add feature1'
cp $WORK/files/feature2.txt feature2.txt
git add feature2.txt
gs branch create feature2 -m 'Add feature2'

git rev-parse --short feature2
cmp stdout $WORK/golden/feature2-before.txt

cp $WORK/files/inserted.txt inserted.txt
git add inserted.txt
gs branch create --below --no-restack inserted -m 'Add inserted'

# feature2 is the target of --below and must not be restacked onto inserted.
git rev-parse --short feature2
cmp stdout $WORK/golden/feature2-before.txt

-- files/feature1.txt --
Feature 1
-- files/feature2.txt --
Feature 2
-- files/inserted.txt --
Inserted
-- golden/feature2-before.txt --
816e420
